displayTitle: Sound You Can't Hear, Flaws You Can't See
kicker: UT LEVEL I // SOUND BEYOND HEARING

beats.narration: Welcome to ultrasonic testing: finding hidden flaws by sending sound deep into the part and listening for the echoes.

payload.title: ULTRASONIC TESTING
payload.sub: Hidden flaws, found by their echoes.

beats.narration: Sound is mechanical vibration. Hearing stops near twenty kilohertz; beyond that is ultrasonic. Industrial flaw detection works mostly between one and ten megahertz.
Around nineteen forty, Firestone's pulse-echo changed everything: send a short pulse, listen with the same crystal. Echo travel time reveals flaw depth, from one side.
Low power listens: finding weld flaws, gauging corroded pipe still in service, catching composite delaminations. High power flips the goal: cleaning parts and welding plastics.

payload.heading: Low power listens — high power changes

beats.narration: From the first flaw detectors to phased arrays, it's still one idea: send a pulse, listen, time the echo.

payload.sub: Still the one idea behind industrial UT.
